title: Add session cleanup utility
description: Create `cleanExpiredSessions(store: SessionStore, maxAgeMs: number): number` in `src/auth/session-cleanup.ts` that removes sessions where `Date.now() - createdAt.getTime() > maxAgeMs` and returns the count of removed sessions. Include co-located tests.
existing_patterns: src/auth/session-store.ts:63 - uses `session.expiresAt <= new Date()` pattern for expiry checks
src/auth/session-store.ts:119-126 - existing `cleanup()` method iterates sessions and revokes by expiry
src/utils/*.ts - utility modules follow single-function file pattern with co-located .test.ts
questions: The task specifies `maxAgeMs` which implies `createdAt`-based age filtering. Should the utility also consider `expiresAt` or `refreshExpiresAt` as secondary conditions, or purely `createdAt` age only?
The existing `cleanup()` removes sessions where BOTH `expiresAt` AND `refreshExpiresAt` are expired. Should this utility use the same dual-expiry logic, or only `createdAt` age?
Should the utility handle sessions with missing/invalid `createdAt` dates gracefully, or is that an impossible state given the SessionStore.create() always sets it?
